weaknesses: No clear Generative AI/LLM project evidence
improvement_bullets: Engineered predictive machine learning models to analyze loan default risk, utilizing feature engineering to identify high-risk segments and support data-driven decisioning for portfolio management.
Developed and deployed end-to-end analytical solutions, ensuring production-ready code through rigorous model governance and documentation of data validation protocols.
Integrated Generative AI tools and LLMs into automated reporting workflows to synthesize complex unstructured data, enhancing the speed of actionable insights for executive stakeholders.
Applied experimental design and statistical optimization techniques to marketing datasets, directly contributing to a 300% increase in customer acquisition through targeted, high-impact campaigns.

Leveraged financial datasets to build scalable analytical workflows, providing the cross-functional team with clean, reliable data products to support commercial strategy and financial performance monitoring.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Senior Analyst-Data Analytics opportunity with American Express. I am interested in this role because I am passionate about leveraging predictive modeling and advanced analytics to drive growth in the commercial services sector. What interests me about working for American Express is its 175-year history of innovation and its commitment to backing its customers through robust data integrity and financial security. I believe my expertise in building end-to-end analytical pipelines and my experience with machine learning and customer segmentation would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my career, I have consistently approached problems by bridging the gap between raw data and strategic decisioning. For example, at Shine Masters, I developed time series models to identify high-value customer segments, which informed targeted marketing initiatives that resulted in a 300% increase in customer acquisition. Ultimately, I know that the Senior Analyst-Data Analytics role at American Express would allow me to bring my technical expertise in Python and SQL to build world-class data products that enhance targeting and acquisition effectiveness. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies (MassMutual) enabled me to develop my production-ready data engineering skills. In that capacity, I collaborated with cross-functional teams to integrate disparate insurance data sources, optimizing relational database queries to improve data accuracy by 30% and streamlining development for the underwriting department. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in financial services and data modeling in working on scalable analytical workflows at American Express. I know that my strong programming foundation and my analytical mindset align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
